Foam

Vector Spaces

There are two important operations within a vector space which contains a set VVV and a field F\mathbb{F}F which itself

  1. Addition : Where we take as input two elements of a set V and return a single elemnt of a set V
  2. Scalar Multiplication : Where we take as input one scalar and one element of a set V and return a single element of a set V.

We want our operations to be closed under addition and scalar multiplication. This means that a sum of two elements in a set must belong to the original set. The same should also hold for a scalar multiple of an eleemtn of the set. When this holds, we say that VVV is closed under addition and closed under scalar multiplication.

Vector Field Properties

  1. Addition is commutative

    \[\forall u,v \in V , u + v = v + u\]

  2. Addition is associative

\[\forall u,v,w \in V, u + (v+w) = (u+v)+ w\]
  1. The set V contains an additive identity
\[\exists \epsilon \in \mathbb{V} \text{ such that } \forall v \in V , v + \epsilon = v\]
  1. The set V contains an additive inverse
\[\forall v \in V , \exists u \in V \text{ such that } u + v = \epsilon\]
  1. Scalar multiplcation is associative
\[\forall \alpha,\beta \in \mathbb{F}, v \in V, \alpha\cdot(\beta v) = \alpha \cdot \beta (v)\]

  1. The field R\mathbb{R}R contains a multiplicative identity such that ∀v∈V,v⋅1=v\forall v \in V, v \cdot 1 = v∀v∈V,v⋅1=v

  2. Operations interact via distributive property.

\[\forall u,v \in V \& \alpha \in \mathbb{F}, \alpha ( u + v) = \alpha \cdot u + \alpha \cdot v\] \[\forall \alpha, \beta \in \mathbb{F} \& v \in V, \alpha\cdot v + \beta \cdot v = (\alpha + \beta) \cdot v\]

Note

  1. Any field F\mathbb{F}F is a vector space over itself. We can also define a set of vectors using the existing field by creating a set of tuples of length nnn containing elements from F\mathbb{F}F. All that matters is that the field operation axioms are satisfied.

Here are some [vector-spaces-proofs] to consider.
